question: What has keys but no locks?
What has hands but can't clap?
hint: It tells you something important every day

question: What has a head and a tail but no body?
hint: You might flip it to make a decision

question: What gets wetter the more it dries?
hint: You use it after a shower

question: What can you catch but not throw?
What has a neck but no head?
What has teeth but cannot bite?
hint: You use it on your hair

question: What runs but never walks?
hint: You can find it in a river

question: What has an eye but cannot see?
What can travel around the world while staying in a corner?
What has legs but doesn't walk?
What can you break without touching it?
What goes up but never comes down?
What is full of holes but still holds water?
hint: You use it to clean dishes

question: What has words but never speaks?
hint: You can find it in a library

question: What has a thumb and four fingers but is not alive?
hint: You wear it when it's cold

question: What comes once in a minute, twice in a moment, but never in a thousand years?
What has cities but no houses, forests but no trees, and water but no fish?
What can fill a room but takes up no space?
hint: Flip a switch to see it

question: What is always in front of you but can't be seen?
I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. I have no body but I come alive with the wind. What am I?
The more you take, the more you leave behind. What am I?
I have branches but no fruit, trunk, or leaves. What am I?
What can you hold in your right hand but never in your left hand?
What is seen in the middle of March and April that can't be seen at the beginning or end of either month?
hint: Look at the spelling of the months

question: What disappears as soon as you say its name?
I am not alive but I grow; I don't have lungs but I need air; I don't have a mouth but water kills me. What am I?
What has one head, one foot, and four legs?
What is lighter than a feather but the strongest person can't hold it for more than five minutes?
I follow you all the time and copy your every move, but you can't touch me or catch me. What am I?
hint: You need light to see me

question: What goes through cities and fields but never moves?
What building has the most stories?
hint: Think about a different meaning of 'stories'

question: I have a face but no eyes, hands but no arms. What am I?
What invention lets you look right through a wall?
What can be cracked, made, told, and played?
What has 13 hearts but no other organs?
What starts with T, ends with T, and has T in it?
hint: It's used to brew a warm drink

question: If you have me, you want to share me. If you share me, you haven't got me. What am I?
What tastes better than it smells?
hint: Think about which body part does the tasting

question: What kind of coat is best put on wet?
I am taken from a mine and shut up in a wooden case, from which I am never released, and yet I am used by almost every person. What am I?

What word in the English language does the following: the first two letters signify a male, the first three letters signify a female, the first four letters signify a great, while the entire word signifies a great woman?
I have cities but no people, forests but no animals, and oceans but no water. What am I?
hint: You might find me on a desk or in a classroom

question: I fly without wings. I cry without eyes. Wherever I go, darkness follows me. What am I?
hint: Look up at the sky on a stormy day

question: What is so fragile that saying its name breaks it?
I can be long or short, grown or bought, painted or left bare. I sit on the ends of your fingers. What am I?
hint: You might paint them or bite them

question: I turn once, what is out will not get in. I turn again, what is in will not get out. What am I?
hint: You use it with a lock

question: What English word retains the same pronunciation even after you remove four of its five letters?
First you eat me, then you get eaten. What am I?
What can run but never walks, has a mouth but never talks, has a head but never weeps, has a bed but never sleeps?
hint: It's a natural feature of the landscape

question: The person who makes it, sells it. The person who buys it never uses it. The person who uses it never knows they're using it. What is it?
hint: It's used after someone passes away

question: What five-letter word becomes shorter when you add two letters to it?
hint: The answer is in the question

question: I am bought by the yard but worn by the foot. What am I?
What word is spelled incorrectly in every dictionary?
I have a spine but no bones. I have pages but no writing of my own. I hold stories but cannot speak. What am I?
hint: You'd find millions of me in a library

question: What belongs to you but other people use it more than you?
What begins with an E and only contains one letter?
hint: You put something inside it to mail

question: I have hands that wave at you, though I never say goodbye. It's cool when I am with you, and yet I am full of hot air. What am I?
hint: You turn me on when it's warm

question: I have no voice but I answer when spoken to. I have no legs but I run away if not held. What am I?
hint: Children play with me in the park

question: I have a bed but never sleep. I have a bank but no money. I run but have no legs. What am I?
What has four wheels and flies?
I wear a crown but am no king. I have roots but do not grow in soil. I sit in your mouth. What am I?
hint: The dentist takes care of me

question: What occurs once in every minute, twice in every moment, yet never in a thousand years?
I have a heart that never beats, a home but I never sleep, I can take a man's house and build another. What am I?
hint: You use me to play games

question: What word looks the same backwards and upside down?
hint: It's something you do in a pool

question: I am an odd number. Take away a letter and I become even. What number am I?
What is 3/7 chicken, 2/3 cat, and 2/4 goat?
hint: Take those fractions of each word's letters

question: I am weightless, but you can see me. Put me in a barrel, and I make it lighter. What am I?
What do you throw out when you want to use it but take in when you don't want to use it?
Poor people have it. Rich people need it. If you eat it you die. What is it?
hint: Think about what poor people have that rich people want

question: What gets broken without being held?
hint: It's something you make, not something physical

question: I'm tall when I'm young and short when I'm old. What am I?
I have no walls, no doors, no windows, yet inside me there is a golden treasure. What am I?
hint: You might have me for breakfast

question: I start out tall but the longer I stand, the shorter I grow. What am I?
hint: You use me to write and draw

question: I have a golden head, a golden tail, but no body. What am I?
hint: You might find me in a treasure chest

question: I am not a bird, but I can fly through the sky. I am not a river, but I carry passengers. What am I?
hint: I take off from a runway

question: What has a ring but no finger?
I have four legs in the morning, two legs at noon, and three legs in the evening. What am I?
hint: Think about the stages of life

question: I can be written, I can be spoken, I can be broken, I can be exposed. What am I?
I drape the hills in white and howl through the night. I can sting your face yet leave no trace. What am I?
hint: You can feel me but not see me

question: I have roots nobody sees, I am taller than trees, up and up I go, yet I never grow. What am I?
hint: Climbers try to reach my peak

question: What is black when it's clean and white when it's dirty?
I am always arriving but never actually arrive. I am always coming but never here. What am I?
hint: When it gets here, it becomes something else

question: I can bring tears to your eyes and resurrect the dead. I form in an instant but last a lifetime. What am I?
hint: You store me in your mind

question: What has a bottom at the top?
What can you keep after giving to someone?
What coat is best put on wet?
What kind of band never plays music?
What has ears but cannot hear?
What room do ghosts avoid?
hint: Think about what ghosts are NOT

question: What kind of tree can you carry in your hand?
I link two people yet I touch only one. What am I?
hint: You wear me on a special finger

question: I sleep by day and fly by night. I have no feathers to aid my flight. What am I?
hint: I hang upside down in caves

question: I have many keys but open no doors. I have space but no room. You can enter but cannot go inside. What am I?
hint: You use me with a computer

question: What has many needles but doesn't sew?
What is easy to get into but hard to get out of?
What can go up a chimney down but can't go down a chimney up?
hint: Think about the shape when open vs closed

question: Forward I am heavy, but backward I am not. What am I?
I can sizzle like bacon, I am made with an egg, I have plenty of backbone but lack a good leg. I peel layers like onions but still remain whole. What am I?
What disappears the moment you start talking about it?
hint: Think about what happens when you speak
